directive_events.owner_directive: Improve SciStudio's overall logging scheme for the upcoming alpha closed-beta so bugs found during testing can be reproduced. Phase 1 (this step): investigate the current logging state — which modules emit logs, where logs are persisted, where logs live in the desktop distribution, whether backend API logs are persisted to disk, whether the frontend persists logs, whether the core engine logs, whether the event bus has an event log. Then identify which modules need logging and quantify the gap.
directive_events.reason: Owner set the round's task: survey and improve SciStudio logging for alpha closed-beta bug reproduction

directive_events.owner_directive: Second task: build a stable version-number management system for the alpha stage. Versions must follow the scheme a.b.c-alpha|beta-build<NNNN> and auto-increment by build. Phase 1 (this step): investigate how versions are currently defined, built, and released. (Note: logging and versioning are unrelated work; whether they ship as one branch/PR or two is pending owner decision.)
directive_events.reason: Owner added a second work direction to the session: version-number management for alpha

directive_events.owner_directive: Versioning decisions: (1) accept dual-track, Python side must be PEP 440 compliant; (2) build number derived from a run number; (3) build number RESETS to zero on alpha->beta channel change; (4) NO GitHub release for now — local build only. OPEN/AMBIGUOUS: since builds are local-only, the 'run number' source for local builds is undefined (GitHub run_number does not exist locally) — to be clarified before versioning implementation, likely a local persistent build counter. Both logging and versioning are in scope this session; owner wants to discuss logging requirements first.

Logging requirements confirmed: (1) layered logging — engine/engine-events, backend API, frontend, desktop — each layer recorded distinctly, all four at the SAME granularity. (2) On-disk location agreed (desktop logs_dir(); project .scistudio/logs/), retention 7 days. (3) Frontend errors POST to backend and are persisted there (no third party). (4) Per-run diagnostic log + a small frontend button for the user to export logs. (5) Both formats required: JSON-line on disk + human-readable console. (6) Redaction: record metadata (paths/type/size/hash), not data contents; config values recordable with sensitive-field redaction. (7) Levels: full info/debug/warning/error. (8) Fine granularity: every call gets a DEBUG log; every user action gets an INFO log; same granularity across all four layers. Versioning: local build number comes from a local persistent counter (resolves the earlier open question).
directive_events.reason: Owner confirmed full logging requirements and the versioning build-counter source

directive_events.owner_directive: FINAL: (1) logging AND versioning ship in ONE combined PR this session (owner overrides the focused-PR split). (2) Implement fine-grained DEBUG via boundary instrumentation only — do NOT broadly rewrite repository functions. (3) Write a spec first, then implement. (4) NEW: the websocket logs the owner normally sees in the terminal must also be persisted to disk. (5) Owner is away/asleep; work autonomously to a finished PR. All operations pre-authorized by owner (incl. protected-core changes, issue creation, branch push, PR creation). Do NOT merge — AI must not self-merge.
directive_events.reason: Owner final decisions: single combined PR, boundary-only debug, spec-first, websocket logs to disk, pre-authorized autonomous work

directive_events.owner_directive: Implement alpha logging (#1741) + versioning (#1742) per the two new specs, one combined PR. Spec-first done; now implementing.
